Adani Group Controversy.title: Adani Group: From Hindenburg Crisis to Full Recovery
Adani Group Controversy.description: The Adani Group story is the most dramatic corporate saga in Indian business history. A single short-seller report wiped out Rs10 lakh crore in 3 days, triggered a Supreme Court probe, and tested the resilience of India's largest infrastructure conglomerate. By 2026, the group has fully recovered and is winning record government contracts.
Adani Group Controversy.mediaAngle: Most media focuses on the stock price recovery but misses the structural shift - Adani has quietly reduced promoter pledge from 35% to 8%, which is the real indicator of financial health.

timeline.event: Hindenburg Research publishes 106-page short report - Rs10 lakh crore wiped in 3 days
timeline.details: The report alleged 88 questions about accounting fraud, stock manipulation, and offshore entities.

timeline.event: Adani Group withdraws Rs20,000 crore FPO citing market conditions
timeline.details: The FPO was fully subscribed but Adani chose to return money to protect investor interests.

timeline.event: GQG Partners invests .87B - first major institutional confidence signal
timeline.details: Rajiv Jain of GQG called it the most mispriced opportunity he had seen in 30 years.

timeline.event: Supreme Court expert committee finds no SEBI regulatory failure
timeline.details: The 6-member committee submitted its report clearing SEBI of any lapses in monitoring Adani stocks.

timeline.event: Hindenburg attacks SEBI Chairperson Madhabi Puri Buch - allegations denied
timeline.details: Hindenburg alleged conflict of interest. SEBI and Buch denied all charges. Government backed SEBI chief.

timeline.event: Hindenburg Research dissolves - founder Nate Anderson cites personal reasons
timeline.details: The closure of Hindenburg removed the most vocal critic of the Adani Group.

timeline.event: Adani Green wins 8 GW SECI tender - largest renewable energy order in India history
timeline.details: The Rs45,000 crore deal cements Adani Green as India's dominant renewable energy player.

timeline.event: Adani Group market cap crosses Rs20 lakh crore - full recovery complete
timeline.details: GQG Partners investment is now worth 3x. Promoter pledge reduced to 8% from 35%.

keyPlayers.name: Gautam Adani
keyPlayers.stance: Led recovery through deleveraging and transparency - reduced group debt by Rs40,000 crore
keyPlayers.recentAction: Announced Rs75,000 crore capex plan for green energy and airports in FY27

keyPlayers.name: Rajiv Jain
keyPlayers.stance: Made the contrarian .87B bet in March 2023 - now sitting on 3x returns
keyPlayers.recentAction: Increased GQG stake in Adani Ports by  in Q1 2026

keyPlayers.name: Nate Anderson
keyPlayers.stance: Published the original short report - dissolved firm in January 2025
keyPlayers.recentAction: Dissolved Hindenburg Research citing personal reasons and burnout

keyPlayers.name: Madhabi Puri Buch
keyPlayers.stance: Cleared of conflict of interest allegations - SEBI investigation into Adani ongoing
keyPlayers.recentAction: SEBI issued show-cause notice to Adani entities in December 2025 on offshore entity disclosures

keyPlayers.name: Karan Adani
keyPlayers.stance: Oversaw the most successful business unit - Adani Ports grew revenue 28% in FY26
keyPlayers.recentAction: Signed MOU with Sri Lanka for Colombo port development worth

sentimentData.label: Near full recovery, institutional buying
sentimentData.event: Market cap crosses Rs15 lakh crore

sentimentData.label: Full recovery, growth mode
sentimentData.event: Market cap crosses Rs20 lakh crore

contrarian.title: The recovery is built on government contracts - not fundamental business improvement
contrarian.argument: While the stock price has recovered, critics argue that Adani Group growth is entirely dependent on government-awarded contracts rather than competitive market wins. The group has won over Rs2 lakh crore in government tenders since 2023, raising questions about whether this is a genuine business recovery or political patronage.
evidence: 85% of Adani Green revenue comes from government PPAs - no market competition
Adani Airports manages 7 of 8 airports under government concession agreements
SEBI show-cause notice in December 2025 suggests regulatory issues are not fully resolved

watchNext.signal: SEBI final order on offshore entity disclosure violations
watchNext.implication: A penalty order could trigger another round of selling - watch for settlement vs contested order

watchNext.signal: Adani Green IPO of subsidiary Adani Solar Energy
watchNext.implication: A successful IPO would validate the renewable energy business and unlock value for investors

watchNext.signal: GQG Partners stake sale in any Adani company
watchNext.implication: Any GQG exit would be a major negative signal - they are the anchor institutional investor

watchNext.signal: New short-seller report from any firm
watchNext.implication: With Hindenburg dissolved, the risk is lower but not zero - Muddy Waters and Gotham City Research are active

Paytm Banking Crisis.title: Paytm Payments Bank: How India Biggest Fintech Hit a Regulatory Wall
Paytm Banking Crisis.description: Paytm Payments Bank crisis is the defining regulatory story of Indian fintech. RBI action in January 2024 effectively shut down Paytm Payments Bank, wiping out Rs30,000 crore in market cap and forcing a complete business model pivot. The story reveals the tension between fintech innovation and regulatory compliance in India.
Paytm Banking Crisis.mediaAngle: Media covered the RBI action extensively but missed the real story: Paytm had been warned 3 times over 2 years about KYC violations and chose to prioritize growth over compliance.

timeline.event: RBI bars Paytm Payments Bank from onboarding new customers - first warning
timeline.details: RBI cited KYC violations and data localisation concerns. Paytm stock fell 15%.

timeline.event: RBI conducts special audit of Paytm Payments Bank - findings kept confidential
timeline.details: The audit reportedly found persistent KYC violations and data sharing with Chinese entities.

timeline.event: RBI orders Paytm Payments Bank to stop all deposits and credit transactions by Feb 29
timeline.details: The order effectively shut down the banking subsidiary. Paytm stock fell 40% in 2 days.

timeline.event: Vijay Shekhar Sharma resigns as Paytm Payments Bank chairman
timeline.details: The resignation was seen as an attempt to distance the parent company from the banking subsidiary.

timeline.event: Paytm migrates payment services to Axis Bank and Yes Bank
timeline.details: The migration was completed in 3 weeks - faster than expected. Payment volumes recovered to 80% of pre-crisis levels.

timeline.event: Paytm reports first quarterly profit since listing - Rs219 crore PAT
timeline.details: The profit was driven by cost cuts and focus on high-margin financial services distribution.

timeline.event: Paytm stock recovers to Rs700 from Rs300 low - still 60% below IPO price
timeline.details: Recovery driven by profitability and new RBI-approved payment aggregator license.

timeline.event: Paytm applies for Small Finance Bank license - new chapter begins
timeline.details: If approved, this would allow Paytm to rebuild its banking business under stricter compliance framework.

keyPlayers.name: Vijay Shekhar Sharma
keyPlayers.stance: Survived the crisis - pivoted company to profitability but lost banking ambitions
keyPlayers.recentAction: Applied for Small Finance Bank license in January 2026

keyPlayers.name: Shaktikanta Das
keyPlayers.stance: Took decisive action citing persistent non-compliance - sent a message to entire fintech sector
keyPlayers.recentAction: Approved Paytm payment aggregator license in August 2024

keyPlayers.name: Softbank
keyPlayers.stance: Held through the crisis - did not sell despite 70% loss from IPO price
keyPlayers.recentAction: Participated in Paytm ESOP buyback program in Q3 2025

keyPlayers.name: Ant Financial
keyPlayers.stance: Sold entire stake in 2023 amid India-China tensions - crystallised massive loss
keyPlayers.recentAction: Fully exited Paytm in 2023 at significant loss

keyPlayers.name: NPCI
keyPlayers.stance: Allowed Paytm to continue UPI operations through third-party app model
keyPlayers.recentAction: Granted Paytm third-party application provider status for UPI in March 2024

sentimentData.label: IPO at Rs2,150 - India largest IPO
First RBI warning - new customer ban

contrarian.title: Paytm recovery is a mirage - the core payments business has permanently lost ground to PhonePe and Google Pay
contrarian.argument: While Paytm has achieved accounting profitability, its UPI market share has fallen from 15% to 8% and continues to decline. The payments business is a commodity with zero switching costs. Without a banking license, Paytm cannot build the high-margin lending business that justifies its valuation.
evidence: UPI market share fell from 15% in Jan 2024 to 8% in Jan 2026 - structural loss
PhonePe and Google Pay now control 85% of UPI transactions between them
Paytm stock at Rs700 is still 67% below its IPO price of Rs2,150 - investors have not recovered

watchNext.signal: RBI decision on Small Finance Bank license application
watchNext.implication: Approval would be transformational - Paytm could rebuild lending business. Rejection would cap upside permanently.
If share stops falling, it signals Paytm has found its floor and can build from there
Softbank selling would signal loss of confidence in recovery story
Sustained profitability would re-rate the stock and attract institutional investors
